title: Init Blocks
description: Run setup code automatically when an object is born.

slides.title: The Birth of an Object 🐣
slides.content:
## Initial Setup

Sometimes, simply taking variables through the constructor isn't enough. 

What if you need to run complex logic, print a welcome message, or evaluate math the exact moment a new object is created?

slides.title: The 'init' Block ⚙️
slides.content: You can use an `init` (initialization) block. The code inside this block is automatically executed the moment the constructor finishes building the object.

quizData.question: When exactly does the code inside an `init` block run?

options.text: Automatically, the moment an object is created from that class.
Only if you manually call it with `object.init()`.

quizData.explanation: Initialization blocks are executed automatically as part of the object construction process.

slides.title: Multiple Init Blocks 🥞
slides.content:
## Stacking them up

If you have a very complex class, you can actually use multiple `init` blocks! 
Kotlin will simply execute them from top-to-bottom in the order they were written inside the class.
